title: AI Integrations: Hidden Data Exfiltration Risks

modules.title: How AI Integrations Create New Data Exposure Pathways
modules.sub: Understanding why AI connectors expand your organization's attack surface
modules.description: Learners examine how AI tools introduce novel data exposure risks that traditional security models were not designed to address. They analyze the architectural decisions — such as granting broad permissions or connecting AI to sensitive internal systems — that transform convenient integrations into potential exfiltration vectors.

modules.title: Mapping Sensitive Data Flows in LLM-Connected Architectures
modules.sub: Tracing where sensitive data travels once AI enters the stack
modules.description: Learners build a structured data-flow map of a representative LLM-connected environment, identifying every point where sensitive information is ingested, processed, or transmitted. They practice labeling trust boundaries and flagging high-risk data paths that lack adequate visibility or control.

modules.title: Attack Scenarios: Prompt Injection, API Abuse, and Shadow AI
modules.sub: Dissecting real-world techniques attackers use to exfiltrate data through AI
modules.description: Learners step through three categories of AI-specific attack scenarios — prompt injection, API credential abuse, and unauthorized shadow AI deployments — analyzing how each technique enables data to leave organizational control. They evaluate case-based breach narratives to recognize attacker tradecraft and identify the defensive gaps each scenario exploits.

modules.title: Detecting Exfiltration Attempts in AI-Augmented Environments
modules.sub: Building detection logic tuned for AI-driven data egress behaviors
modules.description: Learners design detection strategies for identifying anomalous data egress patterns specific to AI-augmented workflows, including unusual query volumes, unexpected external API calls, and atypical prompt outputs. They configure alert criteria and assess how existing SIEM or DLP tooling must be adapted to account for the behavioral signatures of AI-assisted exfiltration.

modules.title: Auditing and Hardening AI Integration Points
modules.sub: Running structured audits to expose and close integration vulnerabilities
modules.description: Learners conduct a structured audit of a simulated AI integration environment, applying a step-by-step checklist to surface misconfigurations, overprivileged service accounts, and unencrypted data channels. They then prioritize remediation actions and implement targeted hardening controls to reduce the attack surface at each identified integration point.

modules.title: Regulatory and Compliance Dimensions of AI Data Leakage
modules.sub: Aligning AI data risk management with legal and regulatory obligations
modules.description: Learners map AI-related data leakage risks against relevant regulatory frameworks — including GDPR, HIPAA, and emerging AI-specific regulations — to understand reporting obligations and compliance exposure. They evaluate a breach scenario and determine which regulatory notifications, documentation requirements, and control mandates would apply to the organization's AI integration posture.

modules.title: Building an AI Data Loss Prevention Playbook
modules.sub: Turning audit findings and controls into an actionable, repeatable DLP strategy
modules.description: Learners synthesize the skills developed across the course into a practical AI Data Loss Prevention playbook tailored to their organization's integration landscape. They define roles, escalation paths, monitoring cadences, and response procedures, producing a living document designed to evolve alongside new AI tool adoptions and emerging threat patterns.

synopsis: When organizations plug AI tools into their workflows, they often unknowingly open new channels for sensitive data to leave the building — through misconfigured APIs, overprivileged integrations, or subtle prompt injection attacks. This course dissects real breach scenarios involving AI integrations and equips security professionals to find, monitor, and close those gaps before attackers do.
rationale: As AI integrations proliferate across enterprise stacks, data exfiltration via these pathways is outpacing existing DLP tooling and awareness — and existing curriculum covers data exposure conceptually without addressing the integration attack surface specifically.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit an AI integration for data exfiltration risk, identify misconfigured data flows, and implement targeted controls to prevent unauthorized data egress through AI-connected systems.
source_signals: Google Antigravity exfiltrates data via indirect prompt injection attack
Paige Thompson convicted over data theft from AWS user accounts
Multi-modal prompt injection image attacks against GPT-4V
nearest_existing: How AI Systems Consume and Expose Personal Data
